meaning: adj.裂片似的,易碎裂的,碎裂的
examples: I could not recognize any of the splintery plant life here.
The hole in the sage-covered hill had been shored up by timbers. They were weathered and splintery, and looked like a picture frame around nothing.
Way too many Italian reds and Spanish Riojas tasted like licking a splintery, dried-out wooden board.
